name: The First National Bank & Trust
description_short: Community bank serving Western Oklahoma since 1901 with locations in Elk City and Sayre, offering personal and business banking, agricultural lending, and loan products.
description_long:
First National Bank & Trust of Elk City and Sayre has been operating in Western Oklahoma for over 120 years, establishing itself as a deeply rooted community financial institution. The bank maintains two physical locations—one in Elk City (with two branch addresses) and one in Sayre—serving the regional farming, ranching, and small business communities. The bank positions itself on local heritage and personalized service rather than competing on digital innovation or national reach.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, business checking and savings accounts, and multiple loan products. Specific offerings include Business First and Business Advantage checking accounts, Money Market Savings and First Reserve savings products, Agricultural Real Estate loans, Livestock financing, Farm Equipment loans, Farm Operating loans, Crop loans, Certificate of Deposit (CD) products, Deposit Account Loans, and Commercial Loans. They also provide Cash Management Services for payroll and billing, IOLTA accounts for law practices, and loan products backed by CDs or deposit accounts for agricultural, commercial, and personal purposes.

The bank differentiates itself through deep agricultural expertise, noting that loan officers "farm and ranch themselves" and understand the seasonal nature of agricultural operations. Their Chief Lending Officer, Jason Redd, is identified as an Elk City native and cattle rancher. The marketing emphasizes personal relationships, local decision-making, and understanding the unique challenges of Western Oklahoma's farming and ranching economy. The brand positioning centers on community connection rather than product breadth or technological sophistication.

As a traditional community bank, First National Bank & Trust operates with the limitations inherent to that model: limited geographic footprint (two small-town locations), likely minimal digital banking innovation, no mention of online account opening or mobile-first features, and limited product innovation. The website provides minimal detail on interest rates, fees, or specific loan terms, requiring customers to contact branches during business hours. For consumers seeking national reach, advanced digital tools, or competitive rate transparency, this is not an optimal choice. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business Checking (Business First and Business Advantage accounts)
Business Savings (Money Market Savings and First Reserve accounts)
Cash Management Services (payroll and billing automation)
Deposit Account Loans (CD-backed loans for agricultural, commercial, and personal purposes)
pros: Agricultural lending specialists with loan officers who personally farm and ranch in the region
Deep community roots dating back to 1901 with established relationships in Western Oklahoma
Wide range of agricultural financing options including real estate, livestock, equipment, and crop loans
Personalized business and personal banking with local decision-making
Specialized services like IOLTA accounts for law practices and Cash Management Services
Flexible Certificate of Deposit and Deposit Account Loan products for multiple purposes
Multiple service channels with two physical locations in Elk City and Sayre
cons: Extremely limited geographic footprint (only Elk City and Sayre locations) restricts accessibility
Website lacks transparency on interest rates, APYs, and fee structures—rates only available 'upon request during business hours'
No mention of online account opening, mobile banking, or digital-first services for remote customers
Limited loan product information with vague descriptions of commercial and CD-backed loans
Small-town focus may mean limited product sophistication, fewer competitive rates, or higher fees compared to regional/national banks

contact.address: 2714 West Third Street, Elk City, OK 73644

best_for: Farmers and ranchers in Western Oklahoma seeking agricultural financing with local expertise
Small business owners in Elk City or Sayre looking for personalized commercial banking
Local residents and business operators who value community relationships and local decision-making
Agricultural operations requiring specialized lending products (equipment, crop, livestock, real estate loans)
diagnosis: First National Bank & Trust is best for farmers, ranchers, and small business owners in Western Oklahoma (Elk City/Sayre area) who prioritize local relationships and agricultural expertise over digital convenience and rate transparency. The main caveat is that this is a hyper-local community bank with minimal online presence, geographic limitations, and opaque pricing—unsuitable for customers outside the service area or those seeking modern digital banking or competitive rate shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 2714 West Third Street, Elk City, OK 73644
